title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Cultivating a Warrior Ethos in the Air Force
summary: RAND researchers examined how the U.S. Air Force can cultivate an Airminded Warrior identity, finding that the concept requires more development. They recommend several actions to strengthen this identity’s development in initial training and beyond.

title: Looking Beyond the Government’s Regulatory Toolkit

title: The Senate Approves the 1795 Jay Treaty
summary: The vote to approve a treaty with Britain set many precedents that govern the treaty-making process to this day and created an irreparable rift between George Washington and Thomas Jefferson.

title: Is General Obscenity Still Illegal? A Postmortem on the Bush Obscenity Prosecution Task Force
summary: Created in 2005, the Bush Administration’s Obscenity Prosecution Task Force (OPTF) marked the last national effort to prosecute obscenity cases. The OPTF successfully prosecuted dozens of cases starting in 2006. President Barack Obama disbanded the task force in 2011, which seemed to end prosecution of general obscenity at the national level. title: Beyond Brexit—and back to Europe
summary: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.

title: Postal Service says its cash crisis is delayed until at least 2031, but problems loom
summary: The U.S. Postal Service is no longer set to be out of cash in 2027, the agency's head says. But its finances remain shaky as Trump officials keep putting it in political hot water.

title: Will Texas' new top voting official be a 'disruptor'? Locals are preparing for it
summary: Just ahead of closely contested midterms, Texas is about to get a new top voting official. Many locals there fear the frontrunner is a state lawmaker and pastor with no election experience.

title: Understanding 'masculinism,' a movement to restore the primacy of men
summary: Masculinism is a belief that feminism emasculates men, and men should be in control while women stay at home raising children. The Atlantic writer Helen Lewis says the movement is becoming mainstream.
